{"key_event":"Post-Cold War drawdown amid carceral expansion","reasoning":"State power remained overwhelming: the Cold War security apparatus shrank somewhat and polarization/devolution weakened federal legitimacy and domestic ambition, but U.S. global military reach, rights-enforcement capacity, and especially policing/prison expansion kept coercive and administrative capacity very high. Society stayed very strong but slipped slightly as declining unions, deindustrialization, welfare retrenchment, media consolidation, and mass incarceration weakened broad counterweights, partly offset by civil-rights mobilization, conservative activism, and early Internet-enabled organization.","society_change":-0.2,"society_power":8.3,"state_change":-0.1,"state_power":9.2}
